meta.name: CD-RISC-10 Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale
meta.description: The CD-RISC-10 is a brief 10-item self-report measure of psychological resilience. It assesses the ability to cope with stress, adversity, and change. Derived from the original 25-item CD-RISC, the 10-item version demonstrates strong psychometric properties and is widely used in clinical, community, and research settings.
meta.author: Campbell-Sills & Stein (2007)
meta.reference: Campbell-Sills L, Stein MB. Psychometric analysis and refinement of the Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale (CD-RISC): Validation of a 10-item measure of resilience. J Trauma Stress. 2007;20(6):1019-1028.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much each statement has been true for you over the past month. If a particular situation has not arisen recently, answer according to how you think you would have dealt with it.

questions.text: I am able to adapt when changes occur
I can deal with whatever comes my way
I try to see the humorous side of things when I am faced with problems
Having to cope with stress can make me stronger
I tend to bounce back after illness, injury, or other hardships
I believe I can achieve my goals, even if there are obstacles
Under pressure, I stay focused and think clearly
I am not easily discouraged by failure
I think of myself as a strong person when dealing with life's challenges and difficulties
I am able to handle unpleasant or painful feelings like sadness, fear, and anger

ranges.label: Low Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience score is low, suggesting significant difficulty coping with stress and adversity. You may feel overwhelmed by challenges and struggle to recover from setbacks.
suggestions: Consider seeking support from a mental health professional
Start building resilience through small, manageable challenges
Practice problem-solving skills in daily situations
Develop a regular self-care routine including exercise and adequate sleep
Connect with supportive people who can help you through difficult times

ranges.label: Moderate Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience is at a moderate level. You can handle some challenges but may struggle with more significant stressors or prolonged adversity.
suggestions: Continue building your coping strategies and stress management skills
Practice reframing negative situations in a more positive light
Engage in regular physical activity to boost mental toughness
Set realistic goals and celebrate small achievements

ranges.label: Moderately High Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience is moderately high. You generally cope well with challenges and can bounce back from most setbacks.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your effective coping strategies
Challenge yourself with new experiences to further build resilience
Share your resilience strategies with others who may benefit
Maintain a balanced lifestyle with adequate rest and recreation

ranges.label: High Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience score is high. You demonstrate strong ability to adapt, cope with stress, and recover from adversity.
suggestions: Consider mentoring others in building resilience
Use your resilience as a foundation for pursuing ambitious goals
Remember to also allow yourself to feel and process difficult emotions
